description: Context recipe for AIWizard mini-app (single-file HTML) generation. Lighter than the macOS-app recipe — no framework scaffold (mini-apps are self-contained HTML). v2 added active-memory layer (Phase MA.3). v3 added excludeTitlePatterns to drop CCAA / AgentRuntime / ScheduledJob log spam from retrieval results. v4 (2026-04-18): added wisdom-framework layer at priority 85 with its own 3500-token budget so framework-specific packs (card-games, arcade-games, visual-quality, etc.) get a dedicated slot instead of competing with universal rules in a single 2500-token pool. v5 (2026-04-19): bumped wisdom-framework to 11000 tokens (~44000 chars) because 3500 tokens was truncating card-games + visual-quality packs combined (30k raw → ~45k rendered) — rules late in the resolve order (cg-011, cg-012, cg-013, cg-020) were being chopped off at '…[truncated]' and the LLM never saw them. v6 (2026-04-19): added layout-reference layer at priority 80 — picks a curated HTML skeleton from assets/layout-references/ matching the goal (hearts/spades/bridge → trick-taking-hearts.html). LLMs follow concrete code patterns far more reliably than they follow 1200-char prose rules; the evaluator can also grep for preserved structural ids. Root cause of the Hearts 'rules visible but ignored' plateau: rules describe, references demonstrate. v7 (2026-04-20): Phase DF — pinned frontend-skill wisdom pack at priority 82 between wisdom-framework (85) and layout-reference (80). Unlike wisdom-framework which is keyword-gated, this pack always loads for miniApp turns so taste rules (hero budget, one-composition, no-cards-by-default, motion recipe, copy discipline) land even when the goal prose contains zero design keywords.
